_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize Houston TX local batch: Harris County DA Sean Teare, City Council members Tarsha Jackson (Dist B), Tiffany Thomas (Dist F), Twila Carter (At-Large 3). Also retire the stale 'Vacant (Special Election Runoff)' District C placeholder — seat filled by Joe Panzarella (D) in the May 16, 2026 runoff (flag: add a proper joe-panzarella record). Local tier, social-media + meeting-record protocol. Every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence.

profile.background: Houston native; UH Law; career Harris County prosecutor who beat incumbent Kim Ogg in the 2024 Dem primary and won the Nov 2024 general. Sworn in Jan 1, 2025. Self-described reform/progressive prosecutor.

sean-teare.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-16 (local tier). Documented reform/progressive DA. LIFE: pro-choice; pledged to 'lobby hard against Republican laws that criminalize abortion' and to deprioritize prosecuting abortion (campaign issues page; ABC13 3/15/2024) -> sanctity_of_life q0/q1 FALSE. PUBLIC JUSTICE: supports cash-bail reform ('cash bail is an antiquated way to keep people in custody') and created a Mental Health & Diversion Bureau diverting nuisance crimes (Texas Scorecard 3/12/2025) -> q1 FALSE; but commits to hard prosecution of violent/capital crime ('you're not going to see violent criminals running the streets') and does NOT excuse violent crime as public health (he applies the public-health framing only to drugs/mental illness) -> q3 TRUE. No affirmative high-bar pro-police advocacy (routine 'work with law enforcement') -> q0 null. GUNS: backs 'common sense gun safety' generically; prosecutes gun crime -> too vague to score the specific self_defense cells -> null. No evidence on marriage, family/education, christian liberty, elections, immigration (Paxton's immigrant-fund suit targets the County, not the DA), or restitution -> null.

0.note: Pro-choice; 'a woman's reproductive healthcare decisions should be between her, her doctor, and no one else.' Does not affirm life/personhood at conception (documented contrary).

1.note: Advocates AGAINST abortion abolition; pledged to 'lobby hard against Republican laws that criminalize abortion' and deprioritize such prosecutions.

0.note: Pledges to 'work closely with law enforcement' (routine for a DA); no affirmative high-bar pro-police advocacy or opposition to defunding found.

1.note: Supports cash-bail reform ('cash bail is an antiquated way to keep people in custody') and created a diversion bureau declining to prosecute nuisance crimes; self-described progressive/reform prosecutor.

2.note: No specific statement on victim restitution found.

3.note: Commits to prosecuting violent/capital crime ('you're not going to see violent criminals running the streets'); applies the public-health/diversion framing only to drugs/mental illness, NOT to violent crime -> does not excuse violent crime as public health. Credit where earned.

4.note: No statement on the magistrate's duty to bear the sword.

profile.background: Houston native; former criminal-justice organizer (Texas Organizing Project Harris County director; co-founder Texas Families of Incarcerated Youth). On Houston City Council District B since Dec 2020; reelected Nov 2023; term ends Jan 2028.

tarsha-jackson.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-16 (local tier). Documented record concentrated in criminal-justice reform and immigration. PUBLIC JUSTICE: led the Harris County bail-reform coalition as TOP director ('incarcerated before guilt... because they can't afford to buy their freedom') -> q1 FALSE; runs/funds 'credible messenger' violence-prevention and campaigned on 'ending mass incarceration' (public-health/diversion framing of violence) -> q3 FALSE; co-sponsored the 2025 HPD traffic-stop quota ban (a reform measure, though HPOU supported it) and otherwise supported the HPD pay raise -> no affirmative high-bar pro-police advocacy and no defund vote -> q0 null. IMMIGRATION: voted FOR the ordinance limiting HPD/ICE cooperation, then voted FOR the weakening compromise (13-4, 4/22/2026) under Gov. Abbott's $110M+ grant threat -> documented pro-sanctuary-limit posture -> border q1/q2 FALSE. No evidence on life, marriage (LGBTQ-chamber reception attendance is association only), family/education, christian liberty, fiscal philosophy (routine budget support only), elections, restitution, or guns (city buyback not tied to her) -> null.

0.note: Reform-oriented (co-sponsored HPD traffic-stop quota ban); supported HPD pay raise but raised process/accountability concerns. No affirmative high-bar pro-police advocacy AND no documented defund vote -> null.

1.note: Led the Harris County bail-reform coalition as TOP criminal-justice director; supports ending wealth-based pretrial detention.

2.note: No statement on victim restitution found.

3.note: Funds 'credible messenger' violence-prevention and campaigned on 'ending mass incarceration' -> embraces a public-health/prevention framing of violence rather than refusing it.

4.note: No statement on the magistrate's duty to bear the sword.

1.note: Voted to limit HPD cooperation with ICE administrative warrants -> opposes deportation cooperation (documented contrary to mandatory-deportation position).

2.note: Backed sanctuary-style HPD/ICE limits (then a fiscal-pressure compromise); supports sanctuary-leaning local policy.

profile.background: Associate Professor of Community Development, Prairie View A&M; chairs the council's Housing & Affordability Committee. First woman to represent Houston District F (Alief/SW Houston); on council since Jan 2020, reelected 2024, term ends Jan 2028.

tiffany-thomas.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-16 (local tier). MARRIAGE/SEXUALITY: endorsed by the Houston GLBT Political Caucus (OutSmart 8/7/2019) -> biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 FALSE (basis: the LGBTQ-advocacy endorsement, HARD evidence of LGBTQ alignment; no specific trans/Pride statement of her own located). NOTE: search engines conflate her with 'Tiffany Tosh' (LGBT Chamber board) — those roles are NOT hers and were excluded. ECONOMIC: voted NO on Mayor Whitmire's ~$7B FY2026 budget (one of 3 dissenters) citing 'more than $70 million in deficit spending' -> economic_stewardship q2 TRUE (documented opposition to deficit spending; her broader objection also included service cuts — scored on the cited deficit-spending objection). REFUSE STATE OVERREACH: asserted local control vs. the governor in the Apr 2026 ICE-ordinance debate ('irresponsible if we're only allowing the governor's office to dictate to us how we lead the city') -> q0 TRUE (content-neutral local-control assertion; context: she opposed state pressure to cooperate with ICE). IMMIGRATION: voted to limit HPD/ICE cooperation and voted NO (one of 4) on the weakening amendment -> border q1/q2 FALSE. GUNS: publicly backed the District F gun-buyback program -> a gun-control posture, but does not map to the specific self_defense cells (red-flag/mag/AWB/registry/constitutional-carry) -> null. No evidence on life, family/education, christian liberty (faith-housing partnership only), elections, bail, or restitution -> null.

0.note: Endorsed by the Houston GLBT Political Caucus — HARD evidence of LGBTQ alignment; does not affirm exclusive man-woman marriage.

2.note: GLBT Political Caucus endorsement (trans-inclusive agenda) indicates she does not reject transgender ideology. Basis is the endorsement, not a specific trans statement.

4.note: GLBT Political Caucus endorsement indicates support for LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

2.note: Voted NO on the FY2026 budget, one of three dissenters, expressly citing 'more than $70 million in deficit spending.' Documented opposition to deficit spending (her objection also cited service cuts; scored on the cited deficit objection).

0.note: Asserted local control against the governor ('irresponsible if we're only allowing the governor's office to dictate to us how we lead the city'). Content-neutral local-control assertion; context: she opposed state pressure to cooperate with ICE.

1.note: Voted to limit HPD/ICE cooperation and against weakening it -> opposes deportation cooperation.

2.note: Pro-sanctuary-leaning: voted to limit ICE cooperation and was one of 4 NO votes on the amendment weakening it.

profile.religion: United Methodist (member, Kingwood United Methodist Church)
profile.background: Former SVP of Community Relations for the Houston Astros and Executive Director of the Astros Foundation. Elected to Houston City Council At-Large Position 3 in the Dec 2023 runoff; term ends Jan 2028.

twila-carter.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-16 (local tier). THIN RECORD — documented HARD positions only on policing and immigration; SILENT (no evidence) on life, marriage, family/education, elections, and guns — notable gaps for a Republican (esp. no pro-life record). PUBLIC JUSTICE: endorsed by the Houston Police Officers Union PAC and campaigned on 'fully funding the Houston Police Department' -> q0 TRUE (affirmative pro-police advocacy). IMMIGRATION: voted AGAINST (one of 5, 12-5, 4/8/2026) the ordinance limiting HPD/ICE coordination; dissenters warned it would 'make officers afraid to do their jobs' -> border q2 TRUE (opposes sanctuary policy). FISCAL: campaign 'fiscal watchdog'/'property taxes are too high' rhetoric + backed the flat-rate budget and stayed out of the 2024 tax-hike faction, but no concrete anti-deficit/balanced-budget vote -> economic_stewardship q2 null (slogans + routine budget support held below the TRUE bar). border q1 (mandatory deportation of all) left null — the anti-sanctuary vote does not specifically establish that position. Faith presence is church membership only -> christian_liberty null. CAVEAT: the 2 scored TRUE cells yield a high headline % off a thin denominator; the silences above are the real story.

0.note: Endorsed by the Houston Police Officers Union PAC; campaigned on 'fully funding the Houston Police Department' and 'safe communities.' Affirmative pro-police advocacy; no defund support.

1.note: No statement on cash bail found.

2.note: No statement on victim restitution found.

3.note: Generic public-safety language only; no crime-as-public-health position.

4.note: No statement on the magistrate's duty to bear the sword.

1.note: No explicit statement supporting mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens; the anti-sanctuary vote does not by itself establish this specific position.

2.note: Voted AGAINST (one of 5) the ordinance limiting HPD/ICE coordination (12-5, 4/8/2026); dissent statement opposed restricting cooperation. Opposes sanctuary policy.

vacant-(special-election-runoff).notes_set: RETIRED 2026-06-16 as a stale placeholder. The Houston City Council District C seat is no longer vacant: Joe Panzarella (D) won the May 16, 2026 special-election runoff (64.7% vs. Nick Hellyar) to fill the remainder of Abbie Kamin's unexpired term (Kamin left to run for Harris County Attorney). FOLLOW-UP FLAG: create a proper 'joe-panzarella' record (D, City Council District C, took office after the 5/16/2026 runoff) rather than publish a real official under this placeholder slug. Sources: Houston.gov District C page; Houston Public Media 5/16/2026; Click2Houston 5/17/2026.
